commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=64ea831a55e0437e498015ad13903a90da8a008e)
commands.review_summary:
This draft PR adds an opt-in dense stream gateway latency harness, cooperative Ollama NDJSON parse yields, and an incremental unphased assistant text streaming path.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +73, Other +590. Total +674 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main has a media-only streamed MEDIA finalization test, and the PR’s new truthy text gate would skip that metadata-only emission path. The dense gateway delay also has PR-body live proof, but Windows and real-Ollama reproduction remain untested here.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The blockers are narrow and actionable, but this is a draft maintainer-labeled PR already waiting on the author, so the next step is author/maintainer repair rather than automated cleanup.

target.title: fix(gateway): keep dense stream updates incremental
target.body:
Opened on behalf of Onur Solmaz (@osolmaz). Work in progress: this is a draft fix/proof PR; Windows and real Ollama-daemon validation are still open proof gaps.

AI-assisted PR. This now includes the production fix plus the deterministic live gateway repro harness for the dense local-provider stream delay.

## Summary

Dense local-provider streams could make the gateway slow to answer unrelated requests.
This keeps ordinary unphased assistant text streaming incremental in the subscriber path, adds cooperative yields while dense Ollama-compatible NDJSON is parsed, and keeps a manual live gateway repro script for before/after proof.

Refs #86599.
Related #86633.

## What Changed

- Added `scripts/repro-gateway-dense-stream-latency.ts` as an opt-in live gateway harness.
- Corrected the repro fixture so the local provider streams chunks in bounded batches instead of blocking the measuring process with one synchronous write loop.
- Added `--assert-responsive`, which checks dense-stream p99 health latency instead of max latency because a one-chunk control run showed unrelated max outliers.
- Updated Ollama native NDJSON parsing to yield cooperatively every bounded set of parsed lines.
- Updated assistant stream subscription so unphased `text_delta` handling uses the new delta and streaming directive accumulator instead of repeatedly extracting/tag-stripping the full partial or accumulated assistant text.
- Kept phase-aware OpenAI Responses behavior on the existing partial-message path.

## Testing

- `node --import tsx scripts/repro-gateway-dense-stream-latency.ts --help`
- `node --import tsx scripts/repro-gateway-dense-stream-latency.ts --chunks 1 --chunk-size 32 --health-timeout-ms 5000`
- `node --import tsx scripts/repro-gateway-dense-stream-latency.ts --chunks 1000 --chunk-size 32 --health-timeout-ms 5000`
- `node --import tsx scripts/repro-gateway-dense-stream-latency.ts`
- `node --import tsx scripts/repro-gateway-dense-stream-latency.ts --assert-responsive`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts scripts/repro-gateway-dense-stream-latency.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

The broad ad hoc scripts tsgo command was not usable because `config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json` currently reports existing `rootDir` errors for all `scripts/**/*.ts` files. I did not use that as pass/fail proof for this PR.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: dense local-provider stream gateway responsiveness delay related to #86599 and #86633.
Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout on `codex/ollama-dense-gateway-repro`, real OpenClaw gateway process, local Ollama-compatible dense NDJSON endpoint. This does not use a real Ollama daemon for the dense output.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx scripts/repro-gateway-dense-stream-latency.ts --assert-responsive`
Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "liveGateway": true,
  "providerProtocol": "ollama-native-compatible",
  "realProviderDaemon": false,
  "chunks": 10000,
  "chunkBytes": 64,
  "serverBatchSize": 100,
  "finalExpectedChars": 640000,
  "chatRequests": 1,
  "sendStatus": "started",
  "runStatus": "ok",
  "ackMs": 138,
  "totalMs": 35917,
  "baseline": {
    "count": 20,
    "min": 34,
    "p50": 37,
    "p95": 48,
    "p99": 48,
    "max": 51,
    "over100ms": 0,
    "over500ms": 0,
    "over1000ms": 0
  },
  "during": {
    "count": 461,
    "min": 29,
    "p50": 32,
    "p95": 316,
    "p99": 358,
    "max": 1388,
    "over100ms": 87,
    "over500ms": 3,
    "over1000ms": 1,
    "errors": 0
  },
  "chatEvents": 85,
  "finalChars": 500000
}
```
Observed result after fix: the dense run completed with p99 health latency `358ms` and no health errors. Max latency still had one `1388ms` outlier; a one-chunk control run also showed a >1s max outlier, so the fixed-branch assertion intentionally uses p99 rather than max.
What was not tested: Windows, WSL2, a real Ollama daemon producing naturally dense output, and a Crabbox/Testbox cross-OS run.

## Risks

- The manual live repro is intentionally opt-in and should not run in normal CI.
- The local endpoint uses the Ollama-compatible native protocol, but it is not a real Ollama daemon.
- The fix preserves phase-aware partial-message handling for OpenAI Responses, but changes unphased streaming to prefer incremental deltas.
target.author: osolmaz
